text.title: Social Proof Types and Placement Strategy
text.summary: Trust level hierarchy by proof type, buying stage mapping, and placement strategy for maximum conversion impact.

rule.statement: Match the proof type to the trust level required at each buying stage, not just what is easiest to collect.
rule.rationale: Showing logo walls to decision-stage visitors and video testimonials to awareness-stage visitors wastes high-trust content on low-intent audiences and leaves decision-stage visitors underserved.

list.item: Video testimonials: highest trust, high effort
Case studies with metrics: very high trust, high effort
Named quotes with photo, role, company: high trust, medium effort
Named quotes without photo: medium trust, low effort
Anonymous reviews: low trust, low effort
Usage numbers ("500+ companies"): medium trust, low effort
Logo walls: medium trust, low effort
Media mentions: medium trust, low effort
Awareness stage: logo walls, media mentions, user count numbers
Consideration stage: case studies, detailed named testimonials
Decision stage: video testimonials, peer review platforms (G2, Capterra)
Purchase stage: guarantees, trust badges, security certifications

rule.statement: Place at least one testimonial within visual proximity of every CTA on the landing page — not only in a dedicated "testimonials section" at the bottom.
rule.rationale: Testimonials reduce purchase anxiety at the moment of decision. A testimonial that requires scrolling to find provides no friction reduction at the CTA location.

list.item: Above the fold: logo wall or user count — quick credibility signal
Next to primary CTA: result-focused quote — reduce friction at conversion
Feature sections: testimonial specific to that feature — contextual relevance
Pricing page: value and ROI testimonials — justify the price
Checkout/signup flow: trust badges, guarantee — final reassurance

rule.statement: Every displayed testimonial must include: full name, company, role, and a real photo. Initials-only attribution and stock photos halve testimonial conversion impact.
rule.rationale: Credibility signals are cumulative. Anonymous or partial attribution reduces believability regardless of quote quality.

list.item: Full name (not initials)
Job title and company name
Real photo (not avatar or stock)
2-3 sentence quote maximum
Bold the specific result if present
